title: State Preserves a Nominal Judiciary While Dismantling Judicial Independence

violations.summary: State officials restructure the judiciary through imposing two-year terms on state trial judges, reducing their salaries mid-term, and abolishing the independent judicial conduct commission in favor of direct legislative discipline, undermining the minimum judicial-independence standards that Article X makes part of the democratic floor.

satisfied_duties.duty: hold that the state's judicial restructuring violates the democratic floor and enjoin the noncompliant measures
satisfied_duties.outcome: held that the state judicial changes violated the democratic-floor minimum standards for terms, compensation, removal protections, and judicial discipline

ai_summary.primary_violation: State officials restructure the judiciary through imposing two-year terms on state trial judges, reducing their salaries mid-term, and abolishing the independent judicial conduct commission in favor of direct legislative discipline, undermining the minimum judicial-independence standards that Article X makes part of the democratic floor.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
